key_metrics: Dialysis patient service revenues of $3.21 billion (+4.8% YoY)
Six-month share repurchases totaling approximately $994 million
qoq_changes: Estimated Q2 revenue of $3.38 billion increased 4.8% sequentially vs implied Q1 2025 revenue of $3.22 billion
Estimated Q2 net income of $199.3 million increased 22.4% sequentially vs implied Q1 2025 net income of $162.9 million
new_risks: Debt expense surged 49.4% YoY to $146.1 million in Q2 2025
Six-month operating cash flow declined 24% YoY to $504.2 million
Long-term debt increased to $10.08 billion from $9.18 billion at year-end 2024
management_tone: Cautiously bullish; core operating performance and top-line growth remain solid, and aggressive share repurchases are successfully driving EPS growth despite the drag of significantly higher debt expenses.
analyst_note: DaVita delivered a mixed but resilient quarter, pairing 6.1% revenue growth and expanded operating margins with a 10.5% drop in net income due to surging interest costs. Aggressive share buybacks masked the net income decline, allowing the company to post a 3.2% increase in diluted EPS.
